016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022
023/**
024 * An <a class="doclink" href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc6901">RFC 6901</a> JSON Pointer that addresses a
025 * single value within a {@link Map}/{@link List} tree (such as the {@link MarshalledMap}/{@link MarshalledList} family).
026 *
027 * <p>
028 * A JSON Pointer is a string of zero or more <c>/</c>-prefixed reference tokens. The empty string <js>""</js> references
029 * the whole document (the root). A non-empty pointer must begin with <c>/</c>; each token is the text between <c>/</c>
030 * separators with <c>~1</c> decoded to <c>/</c> and <c>~0</c> decoded to <c>~</c>. For arrays a token is either a
031 * base-10 non-negative integer index or the single character <c>-</c> (which references the nonexistent element after
032 * the last element, used to append on writes); for objects the decoded token is the member name.
033 *
034 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
035 * <p class='bjava'>
036 *    JsonMap <jv>doc</jv> = JsonMap.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"foo"</js>, JsonList.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"bar"</js>, <js>"baz"</js>));
037 *
038 *    <jc>// Read.</jc>
039 *    Object <jv>v</jv> = JsonPointer.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"/foo/0"</js>).eval(<jv>doc</jv>);  <jc>// "bar"</jc>
040 *
041 *    <jc>// Auto-vivifying write (creates intermediate containers).</jc>
042 *    JsonPointer.<jsm>of</jsm>(<js>"/foo/-"</js>).set(<jv>doc</jv>, <js>"qux"</js>);     <jc>// appends "qux"</jc>
043 * </p>
044 *
045 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
046 *    <li class='warn'>This class is not thread safe.
047 *    <li>Read-miss (missing key, out-of-range index, or type mismatch) resolves to <jk>null</jk> via {@link #eval(Object)}.
048 * </ul>
049 *
050 * <p>
051 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation typed tree model layered over the
052 * {@code Marshalled*} collections and may change incompatibly in a future release.
053 */
054public class JsonPointer {
055
056   /** Sentinel distinguishing an absent value from a present <jk>null</jk> value during traversal. */
057   private static final Object MISSING = new Object();
058
059   /**
060    * Parses and validates the specified RFC 6901 JSON Pointer string.
061    *
062    * @param pointer The pointer string. The empty string references the whole document; a non-empty pointer must begin with <c>/</c>.
063    * @return A new {@link JsonPointer}, never <jk>null</jk>.
064    * @throws IllegalArgumentException If the pointer is <jk>null</jk> or malformed (non-empty and not beginning with <c>/</c>).
065    */
066   public static JsonPointer of(String pointer) {
067      return new JsonPointer(parse(pointer));
068   }
069
070   /**
071    * Encodes a raw object member name into an RFC 6901 reference token (escapes <c>~</c> to <c>~0</c> and <c>/</c> to <c>~1</c>).
072    *
073    * @param rawMemberName The raw member name. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
074    * @return The encoded reference token.
075    */
076   public static String encodeToken(String rawMemberName) {
077      return rawMemberName.replace("~", "~0").replace("/", "~1");
078   }
079
080   /**
081    * Decodes an RFC 6901 reference token into a raw object member name (unescapes <c>~1</c> to <c>/</c> then <c>~0</c> to <c>~</c>).
082    *
083    * @param token The reference token. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
084    * @return The decoded member name.
085    */
086   public static String decodeToken(String token) {
087      return token.replace("~1", "/").replace("~0", "~");
088   }
089
090   private static List<String> parse(String pointer) {
091      if (pointer == null)
092         throw iaex("JSON Pointer cannot be null.");
093      if (pointer.isEmpty())
094         return List.of();
095      if (pointer.charAt(0) != '/')
096         throw iaex("Invalid JSON Pointer ''{0}'': a non-empty pointer must begin with ''/''.", pointer);
097      var parts = pointer.split("/", -1);
098      var tokens = new ArrayList<String>(parts.length - 1);
099      for (var i = 1; i < parts.length; i++)
100         tokens.add(decodeToken(parts[i]));
101      return tokens;
102   }
103
104   private final List<String> tokens;
105
106   private JsonPointer(List<String> tokens) {
107      this.tokens = tokens;
108   }
109
110   /**
111    * Reads the value addressed by this pointer from the specified root.
112    *
113    * <p>
114    * Walks the tokens from <c>root</c> (which may be a {@link Map}/{@link List} such as the {@link MarshalledMap}/
115    * {@link MarshalledList} family). The empty pointer returns <c>root</c>.
116    *
117    * @param root The root document. Can be <jk>null</jk>.
118    * @return The addressed value, or <jk>null</jk> on any missing key, out-of-range index, or type mismatch.
119    */
120   public Object eval(Object root) {
121      var o = evalOrMissing(root);
122      return o == MISSING ? null : o;
123   }
124
125   /**
126    * Writes the specified value at the location addressed by this pointer, auto-vivifying missing intermediate containers.
127    *
128    * <p>
129    * Intermediate containers are created based on the next token: a numeric or <c>-</c> token implies a {@link JsonList},
130    * otherwise a {@link JsonMap}. The <c>-</c> token on a list appends. The empty pointer cannot mutate <c>root</c> in
131    * place for the whole-document case, so it simply returns <c>value</c> as the new root.
132    *
133    * @param root The root document. For a non-empty pointer, must be a mutable {@link Map}/{@link List} tree.
134    * @param value The value to set.
135    * @return The (possibly newly-created) effective root.
136    */
137   public Object set(Object root, Object value) {
138      if (tokens.isEmpty())
139         return value;
140      var parent = navigateToParent(root);
141      rawSet(parent, tokens.get(tokens.size() - 1), value);
142      return root;
143   }
144
145   /**
146    * Removes the member or element addressed by this pointer.
147    *
148    * @param root The root document.
149    * @return The removed value, or <jk>null</jk> if the addressed member/element was absent (or the pointer is the root pointer).
150    */
151   public Object remove(Object root) {
152      if (tokens.isEmpty())
153         return null;
154      var parent = root;
155      for (var i = 0; i < tokens.size() - 1; i++) {
156         parent = rawGet(parent, tokens.get(i));
157         if (parent == MISSING)
158            return null;
159      }
160      return rawRemove(parent, tokens.get(tokens.size() - 1));
161   }
162
163   @Override /* Overridden from Object */
164   public String toString() {
165      if (tokens.isEmpty())
166         return "";
167      var sb = new StringBuilder();
168      for (var t : tokens)
169         sb.append('/').append(encodeToken(t));
170      return sb.toString();
171   }
172
173   /**
174    * Returns <jk>true</jk> if this is the root pointer (the empty pointer addressing the whole document).
175    */
176   boolean isRoot() {
177      return tokens.isEmpty();
178   }
179
180   /**
181    * Same as {@link #eval(Object)} but returns the {@link #MISSING} sentinel (rather than <jk>null</jk>) for an absent
182    * value, so callers can distinguish a present <jk>null</jk> value from a read-miss.
183    */
184   Object evalOrMissing(Object root) {
185      var current = root;
186      for (var token : tokens) {
187         current = rawGet(current, token);
188         if (current == MISSING)
189            return MISSING;
190      }
191      return current;
192   }
193
194   /** Returns <jk>true</jk> if the specified value is the absent-value sentinel returned by {@link #evalOrMissing(Object)}. */
195   static boolean isMissing(Object o) {
196      return o == MISSING;
197   }
198
199   private Object navigateToParent(Object root) {
200      var current = root;
201      for (var i = 0; i < tokens.size() - 1; i++) {
202         var token = tokens.get(i);
203         var child = rawGet(current, token);
204         if (! (child instanceof Map || child instanceof List)) {
205            child = isIndexToken(tokens.get(i + 1)) ? new JsonList() : new JsonMap();
206            rawSet(current, token, child);
207         }
208         current = child;
209      }
210      return current;
211   }
212
213   private static Object rawGet(Object container, String token) {
214      if (container instanceof Map<?,?> container2)
215         return container2.containsKey(token) ? container2.get(token) : MISSING;
216      if (container instanceof List<?> container2) {
217         var idx = toArrayIndex(token);
218         return (idx >= 0 && idx < container2.size()) ? container2.get(idx) : MISSING;
219      }
220      return MISSING;
221   }
222
223   @SuppressWarnings({
224      "unchecked" // Object-node maps accept String keys / Object values; array-node lists accept Object elements.
225   })
226   private static void rawSet(Object container, String token, Object value) {
227      if (container instanceof Map) {
228         ((Map<String,Object>)container).put(token, value);
229         return;
230      }
231      if (container instanceof List) {
232         var list = (List<Object>)container;
233         if ("-".equals(token)) {
234            list.add(value);
235            return;
236         }
237         var idx = toArrayIndex(token);
238         if (idx < 0 || idx > list.size())
239            throw iaex("Invalid JSON Pointer array index ''{0}'' for a list of size {1}.", token, list.size());
240         if (idx == list.size())
241            list.add(value);
242         else
243            list.set(idx, value);
244         return;
245      }
246      throw iaex("Cannot set value at token ''{0}'' on a non-container value.", token);
247   }
248
249   private static Object rawRemove(Object container, String token) {
250      if (container instanceof Map<?,?> container2)
251         return container2.containsKey(token) ? container2.remove(token) : null;
252      if (container instanceof List<?> container2) {
253         var idx = toArrayIndex(token);
254         return (idx >= 0 && idx < container2.size()) ? container2.remove(idx) : null;
255      }
256      return null;
257   }
258
259   private static boolean isIndexToken(String token) {
260      return "-".equals(token) || toArrayIndex(token) >= 0;
261   }
262
263   /*
264    * Returns the non-negative array index for the specified token, or -1 if the token is not a valid RFC 6901 array
265    * index (no leading zeros except "0" itself; "-" is not a numeric index).
266    */
267   private static int toArrayIndex(String token) {
268      var len = token.length();
269      if (len == 0)
270         return -1;
271      if (len > 1 && token.charAt(0) == '0')
272         return -1;
273      for (var i = 0; i < len; i++) {
274         var c = token.charAt(i);
275         if (c < '0' || c > '9')
276            return -1;
277      }
278      try {
279         return Integer.parseInt(token);
280      } catch (NumberFormatException e) {  // HTT - guards against overflow on very long digit strings.
281         return -1;
282      }
283   }
284}
